---
title: "Flu Surveillance Sample Dashboard"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

# Install packages if needed
pkgs <- c("readxl", "dplyr", "lubridate", "plotly", "crosstalk",
          "stringr", "tidyr", "shiny")
to_install <- pkgs[!pkgs %in% installed.packages()[, "Package"]]
if (length(to_install)) install.packages(to_install, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")

library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(plotly)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)
```

```{r load-data}
# в”Ђв”Ђ Load & clean в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
raw <- read_excel("flu_surveillance_samples_260612.xlsx", sheet = 1)

df <- raw %>%
  # De-duplicate to one row per accession (each accession may have multiple
  # analyte rows; keep the first occurrence for counting purposes)
  distinct(PHLAccessionNumber, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    # Excel serial dates в†’ R Date
    SpecimenDateReceived = as.Date(as.numeric(SpecimenDateReceived),
                                   origin = "1899-12-30"),
    # Trim whitespace from factor columns
    across(c(SubmitterName, SubmitterCounty, PatientAddressCounty),
           ~ str_squish(as.character(.x))),
    # Replace blank strings with "Unknown"
    across(c(SubmitterName, SubmitterCounty, PatientAddressCounty),
           ~ if_else(.x == "" | is.na(.x), "Unknown", .x))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(SpecimenDateReceived))

date_min <- min(df$SpecimenDateReceived)
date_max <- max(df$SpecimenDateReceived)
```

```{r ui-controls}
# в”Ђв”Ђ Controls (rendered as plain HTML widgets via ojs / htmltools) в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
# We embed all controls + the plot in one self-contained HTML page using
# Plotly + vanilla JS so no Shiny server is required.
```

```{r build-plot, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
# в”Ђв”Ђ Helper: aggregate data for a given time interval and metadata variable в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
build_bars <- function(data, interval, meta_var) {

  # Floor dates to the chosen interval
  data <- data %>%
    mutate(
      time_bin = floor_date(SpecimenDateReceived,
                            unit = switch(interval,
                                          "Day"   = "day",
                                          "Week"  = "week",
                                          "Month" = "month",
                                          "day"))
    )

  # Count per time_bin Г— metadata category
  agg <- data %>%
    group_by(time_bin, category = .data[[meta_var]]) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

  # Total per bin (for proportion tooltip)
  totals <- agg %>%
    group_by(time_bin) %>%
    summarise(total = sum(n), .groups = "drop")

  agg <- agg %>%
    left_join(totals, by = "time_bin") %>%
    mutate(prop = round(n / total * 100, 1),
           tooltip = paste0(category, "<br>n = ", n,
                            " (", prop, "% of ", total, ")"))

  agg
}

# в”Ђв”Ђ Pre-build all combinations (interval Г— meta_var) for JS toggling в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
intervals <- c("Day", "Week", "Month")
meta_vars <- c("SubmitterName", "SubmitterCounty", "PatientAddressCounty")

# Assign a consistent color palette per category across all combinations
all_cats <- list(
  SubmitterName        = sort(unique(df$SubmitterName)),
  SubmitterCounty      = sort(unique(df$SubmitterCounty)),
  PatientAddressCounty = sort(unique(df$PatientAddressCounty))
)

# Use a qualitative palette (Plotly Safe)
pal_fun <- function(n) {
  cols <- c("#1f77b4","#ff7f0e","#2ca02c","#d62728","#9467bd",
            "#8c564b","#e377c2","#7f7f7f","#bcbd22","#17becf",
            "#aec7e8","#ffbb78","#98df8a","#ff9896","#c5b0d5",
            "#c49c94","#f7b6d2","#c7c7c7","#dbdb8d","#9edae5")
  rep_len(cols, n)
}

color_maps <- lapply(all_cats, function(cats) {
  setNames(pal_fun(length(cats)), cats)
})

# Build all traces as a nested list, serialised to JSON for JS
all_traces <- list()
for (iv in intervals) {
  for (mv in meta_vars) {
    key <- paste(iv, mv, sep = "__")
    agg <- build_bars(df, iv, mv)
    cmap <- color_maps[[mv]]
    cats <- all_cats[[mv]]
    traces <- lapply(cats, function(cat) {
      sub <- agg %>% filter(category == cat)
      list(
        x        = as.character(sub$time_bin),
        y        = sub$n,
        text     = sub$tooltip,
        name     = cat,
        color    = cmap[[cat]],
        visible  = FALSE   # JS will flip these
      )
    })
    # Filter out empty traces (category never appears in this interval)
    traces <- Filter(function(t) length(t$x) > 0, traces)
    all_traces[[key]] <- traces
  }
}

# в”Ђв”Ђ Serialise to JSON (embedded in the HTML page) в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
traces_json <- jsonlite::toJSON(all_traces, auto_unbox = TRUE, null = "null")
```

```{r output-html, results='asis'}
# в”Ђв”Ђ Embed everything as a single self-contained HTML block в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
cat(sprintf('
<style>
  .ctrl-row   { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:20px; flex-wrap:wrap;
                margin-bottom:12px; font-family:sans-serif; }
  .ctrl-group { display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:4px; }
  label       { font-size:13px; font-weight:600; color:#444; }
  select      { padding:5px 10px; border:1px solid #ccc; border-radius:4px;
                font-size:13px; background:#fff; cursor:pointer; }
  select:focus{ outline:2px solid #1f77b4; }
  #flu-plot   { width:100%%; height:520px; }
</style>

<div class="ctrl-row">
  <div class="ctrl-group">
    <label for="iv-sel">Time Interval</label>
    <select id="iv-sel">
      <option value="Day">Day</option>
      <option value="Week" selected>Week</option>
      <option value="Month">Month</option>
    </select>
  </div>
  <div class="ctrl-group">
    <label for="mv-sel">Colour Bars By</label>
    <select id="mv-sel">
      <option value="SubmitterName">Submitter Name</option>
      <option value="SubmitterCounty">Submitter County</option>
      <option value="PatientAddressCounty">Patient Address County</option>
    </select>
  </div>
</div>

<div id="flu-plot"></div>

<script src="https://cdn.plot.ly/plotly-2.27.0.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function() {
  const ALL_TRACES = %s;

  function buildPlotlyTraces(iv, mv) {
    const key  = iv + "__" + mv;
    const list = ALL_TRACES[key] || [];
    return list.map(t => ({
      x:           t.x,
      y:           t.y,
      text:        t.text,
      name:        t.name,
      marker:      { color: t.color },
      type:        "bar",
      hovertemplate: "%%{text}<extra></extra>",
    }));
  }

  const layout = {
    barmode: "stack",
    xaxis: {
      title: { text: "Specimen Date Received", font: { size: 13 } },
      type:  "category",
      tickangle: -35,
      automargin: true
    },
    yaxis: {
      title: { text: "Number of Samples", font: { size: 13 } },
      rangemode: "tozero"
    },
    legend: {
      orientation: "v",
      x: 1.01, y: 1,
      bgcolor: "rgba(255,255,255,0.8)",
      bordercolor: "#ddd",
      borderwidth: 1,
      font: { size: 11 }
    },
    margin: { l:60, r:200, t:30, b:90 },
    plot_bgcolor:  "#fafafa",
    paper_bgcolor: "#ffffff",
    hoverlabel: { align: "left" }
  };

  const config = { responsive: true, displayModeBar: true,
                   modeBarButtonsToRemove: ["lasso2d","select2d"] };

  // Initial render
  Plotly.newPlot("flu-plot",
    buildPlotlyTraces("Week", "SubmitterName"),
    layout, config);

  function refresh() {
    const iv = document.getElementById("iv-sel").value;
    const mv = document.getElementById("mv-sel").value;
    Plotly.react("flu-plot", buildPlotlyTraces(iv, mv), layout, config);
  }

  document.getElementById("iv-sel").addEventListener("change", refresh);
  document.getElementById("mv-sel").addEventListener("change", refresh);
})();
</script>
', traces_json))
```
